commit.message:
fix: VRAM estimation from catalog + LLM re-plan fallback for failed edits

model_selector: Populate _VRAM_MAP and _MAX_CTX from model_catalog instead
of maintaining a separate hardcoded list. Add qwq:32b, qwen3:32b, and
devstral to COMPLEX_MODELS tier. Improve the fallback heuristic to parse
parameter counts from model names (e.g. 'qwq:32b' → ~20 GiB).

agent_loop: Add LLM-assisted re-plan as a third fallback in _act_edit_file.
When both exact and fuzzy matching fail (common with reasoning models like
qwq:32b that hallucinate file contents during planning), the agent now sends
the actual file content to the LLM and asks it to produce corrected old/new
text before giving up.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(jit): zero-cost context swap via Rc::into_raw/from_raw for closure calls

Eliminate atomic Rc inc/dec on every closure call by caching a raw
pointer with one refcount in CachedCalleeEntry. Context swaps use
Rc::from_raw → mem::replace → Rc::into_raw, avoiding 2 atomic ops
per call (~1000 calls in closure_counter benchmark).

Also cache skip_args flag to avoid parameter_count() virtual call
per iteration for 0-param closures.

fix: set ENV PORT to match EXPOSE in web/user-dashboard/game-arena Dockerfiles

Railway routes traffic to the EXPOSE port. These services had EXPOSE set
to their default ports (3000/6001/8080) but Railway was injecting PORT=3001,
causing the app to listen on 3001 while Railway routed to the EXPOSE port.
Set ENV PORT to match EXPOSE so Railway injects the correct routing port.

commit.message:
feat: add lightspeed commands to skip update check for fast exit (#7629)

* feat: add lightspeed commands to skip update check for fast exit

Extensions using AzureDeveloperCLICredential spawn 'azd auth token' as a
subprocess with a 10-second timeout. The background update check goroutine
(60s timeout) was blocking process exit in main.go, causing 'signal: killed'
errors when the update check was slow (e.g., after laptop wake, DNS stalls).

This change adds a Lightspeed property to ActionDescriptorOptions that marks
commands requiring fast exit. The update check goroutine is now started inside
ExecuteWithAutoInstall after command identification — lightspeed commands skip
it entirely, so the process exits immediately after producing output.

Changes:
- Add Lightspeed bool field to ActionDescriptorOptions
- Propagate lightspeed to cobra annotations in CobraBuilder
- Move fetchLatestVersion from main.go into startUpdateCheck() in auto_install.go
- Start update check only after rootCmd.Find(), skip for lightspeed commands
- Return ExecuteResult from ExecuteWithAutoInstall with LatestVersion channel
- Mark 'auth token' as Lightspeed: true

Fixes #7360

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: add lightspeed to cspell dictionary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Restore parse-error log for invalid AZD_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK values

- Set AZD_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK in test to avoid real HTTP calls

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct LatestVersion doc comment to match behavior

The channel is nil only for lightspeed commands (goroutine never starts).
When AZD_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK skips the check, the channel is closed without
a value rather than being nil.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: avoid real credential stack in lightspeed detection test

Use --output unknown to fail fast before touching the credential/provider
stack. The test only needs to verify IsLightspeed detection, not actual
token acquisition.

Directly test the core behavioral change: lightspeed commands must have
LatestVersion == nil (no goroutine started), non-lightspeed commands must
have LatestVersion != nil (goroutine started, even when AZD_SKIP_UPDATE_CHECK
short-circuits it).

Define AnnotationLightspeed = "azd.lightspeed" in cmd/actions package

commit.message:
[Clocktower] Define turn state types and game phases (#450)

Defines the TypeScript types for Clocktower's turn state and phase
system as the foundational layer for the Clocktower game mode
implementation.

### New files

- `src/lib/game/modes/clocktower/types.ts` — all turn state and phase
types
- `src/lib/game/modes/clocktower/index.ts` — barrel export

### Types

- **`ClocktowerPhase` enum** — `Night | Day` discriminant
- **`ClocktowerNightPhase` / `ClocktowerDayPhase`** — discriminated
union members; `ClocktowerNightPhase` tracks `currentActionIndex` and
`nightActions` (keyed by role ID); `ClocktowerDayPhase` carries
`nominations`, `nominatedByPlayerIds`, and `executedToday`
- **`ClocktowerTurnPhase`** — `ClocktowerNightPhase |
ClocktowerDayPhase` union
- **`ClocktowerNightAction`** — primary target record for a role's night
action; intentionally minimal since Clocktower night resolution is
Storyteller-mediated rather than system-computed
- **`ClocktowerNomination` / `ClocktowerNominationVote`** — nomination
with per-player vote tally, supporting ghost votes and sequential public
voting
- **`ClocktowerTurnState`** — full turn state capturing `playerOrder`
(for neighbor abilities), `deadPlayerIds`, `ghostVotesUsed`,
`poisonedPlayerId`, `drunkPlayerId`, and `demonPlayerId`

### Notes

**Day-scoped fields on `ClocktowerDayPhase`**: `nominations`,
`nominatedByPlayerIds`, and `executedToday` live on the day phase rather
than top-level `ClocktowerTurnState` — they are day-scoped data that
naturally resets each phase transition.

**`poisonedPlayerId` and `drunkPlayerId`** are modeled separately:
poisoning is transient (one night + following day), while
`drunkPlayerId` is persistent and hidden from the affected player.

```ts
// Phase narrowing example
function handlePhase(state: ClocktowerTurnState) {
  if (state.phase.type === ClocktowerPhase.Night) {
    // state.phase.nightActions, state.phase.currentActionIndex available
  } else {
    // Day: state.phase.nominations, state.phase.executedToday available
  }
}
```

  a second checkout for the same credit amount. Now logs an error and returns
  early instead of risking incorrect crediting.

  admin double-submission from crediting a user twice. Admin UI can pass a
  frontend-generated UUID as idempotency_key on the POST body.

- admin.py CreditAdjustRequest: expose idempotency_key field so frontend can
  pass it through.

- credit_service.adjust_credits: add with_for_update() lock on user row
  (consistency with add_credits and deduct_credits).

commit.message:
fix: atomic webhook claim + credit row-lock to eliminate race conditions

- billing_service: replace is_event_processed+mark_event (check-then-insert,
  not race-safe) with claim_webhook_event (INSERT, catch IntegrityError) +
  update_webhook_status (UPDATE only). Two concurrent Stripe retries for the
  same event_id can no longer double-process: DB UNIQUE constraint on
  stripe_event_id is the synchronization gate.

- billing router: use claim_webhook_event/update_webhook_status. Legacy
  is_event_processed/mark_event kept as deprecated aliases.

- credit_service.add_credits: SELECT user with with_for_update() to prevent
  concurrent credit additions from reading stale balance.

- credit_service.deduct_credits: re-fetch user with with_for_update() inside
  the deduction transaction instead of trusting the potentially-stale caller-
  provided user object. Prevents concurrent AI requests from both passing the
  balance check and double-deducting.

- tests/test_credits.py: update mocks to reflect the re-fetch pattern
  (side_effect list instead of single return_value).

All 12 tests pass.

campaign: bump target F1 0.95→0.99, add SWA support, fix arch key bug + state reconciliation

Key changes:
- ml_metaopt_campaign.yaml: target_metric updated to 0.99 (from 0.95)
  - Updated comment to reflect measured PP ceiling of 0.9888
- scripts/train.py: add optional SWA phase (swa_start_epoch/swa_epochs/swa_lr config)
  - Activates post-convergence SWA averaging when configured
  - Reverts to pre-SWA best if SWA model is worse by >0.02

Critical bug fixed (in prior session): model.py config key 'arch' (not 'conv_type')
was causing all TransformerConv runs to silently train GATConv. Fixing jumped F1
from 0.86 → 0.9792.

Architecture ceiling confirmed: TransformerConv 5L/128h/4heads (3.44M params)
converges to F1=0.977-0.979 regardless of model size (3M-22M), loss, or LR.

  refactor, resolve, correct, patch, rewrite, configure, test, run, verify,
  validate, confirm, identify, re-run, ensure

  instead of producing 0-step heal plans

Fix MICROSOFT_TENANT_ID being overwritten by login tenant

The auto-discover logic was overwriting the user-provided
MICROSOFT_TENANT_ID with the Azure login tenant ID. Now it only
auto-discovers when MICROSOFT_TENANT_ID is not set in .env, so the
Step 4 tenant comparison correctly detects the mismatch and switches
to the M365 tenant before creating the app registration.
